word_list.word: as a tree falls, so shall it lie
word_list.pronunciation: æz ɐ tɹˈiː fˈɔːlz sˌoʊ ʃˌæl ɪt lˈaɪ

senses.definition: used to imply that the consequences of one's actions or decisions are inevitable and cannot be avoided, and that people must accept the outcome of their choices, whether positive or negative

word_list.word: a good beginning makes (for|) a good ending
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈʊd bɪɡˈɪnɪŋ mˌeɪks fɔːɹ ɐ ɡˈʊd ˈɛndɪŋ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of preparation, planning, and taking the time to ensure that the start of a task or project is done well, as it could lead to a successful outcome

word_list.word: after a storm comes a calm
word_list.pronunciation: ˈæftɚɹ ɐ stˈoːɹm kˈʌmz ɐ kˈɑːm

senses.definition: used to encourage and remind one that even the most challenging situations are temporary and that there is always the possibility of a brighter future

word_list.word: the bigger they are, the (harder|furthest||deeper) they (shall|) fall
word_list.pronunciation: ðə bˈɪɡɚ ðeɪ ɑːɹ ðə hˈɑːɹdɚ fˈɜːðəst dˈiːpɚ ðeɪ ʃˌæl fˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to suggest that those who are in positions of power or authority are often more vulnerable to failure or defeat because they have more to lose

word_list.word: brew, so shall you bake
word_list.pronunciation: bɹˈuː sˌoʊ ʃˌæl juː bˈeɪk

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of taking responsibility for one's actions and recognizing that the effort put in at the outset can significantly impact the eventual outcome

word_list.word: circumstances (often|tend to|) alter cases
word_list.pronunciation: sˈɜːkəmstˌænsᵻz ˈɔfən tˈɛnd tʊ ˈɑːltɚ kˈeɪsᵻz

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of taking into account the specific circumstances of a situation and making decisions based on those circumstances

word_list.word: every why (has|hath) a wherefore
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi wˌaɪ hɐz hæθ ɐ wˈɛɹfoːɹ

senses.definition: used to say that every action or event has a cause or reason behind it, and that understanding the cause of an event can help one to understand its effects

word_list.word: evil be to him who evil (thinks|thinketh)
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːvəl biː tə hˌɪm hˌuː ˈiːvəl θˈɪŋks θˈɪŋkəθ

senses.definition: used to say that those who have malicious or negative intentions toward others will ultimately bring harm upon themselves

word_list.word: no cure no pay
word_list.pronunciation: nˈoʊ kjˈʊɹ nˈoʊ pˈeɪ

senses.definition: used to imply that a person or entity will only receive payment if they are successful in achieving a specific outcome or result

word_list.word: nothing comes out of the sack but what was in it
word_list.pronunciation: nˈʌθɪŋ kˈʌmz ˌaʊɾəv ðə sˈæk bˌʌt wˈʌt wʌz ɪn ɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that the outcome or result of a situation is determined by the factors that were present from the beginning

examples.x: If you want to excel in your career, you must first invest time and effort in developing your skills and knowledge, because nothing comes out of the sack but what was in it.

word_list.word: the sooner begun, the sooner done
word_list.pronunciation: ðə sˈuːnɚ bɪɡˈʌn ðə sˈuːnɚ dˈʌn

senses.definition: used to suggest that procrastination or delay will only prolong the time it takes to complete a task or project

word_list.word: what you have never had you never miss
word_list.pronunciation: wˌʌt juː hæv nˈɛvɚ hɐd juː nˈɛvɚ mˈɪs

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is easier to live without something when one has never experienced it or owned it before, and that one should focus on the things that one does have instead

word_list.word: easy come, easy go
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːzi kʌm ˈiːzi ɡoʊ

senses.definition: used to suggest that when something is obtained or achieved easily, it is often lost or wasted just as easily

word_list.word: the bleating of the kid excites the tiger
word_list.pronunciation: ðə blˈiːɾɪŋ ʌvðə kˈɪd ɛksˈaɪts ðə tˈaɪɡɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that even innocent or unintended actions can have unintended consequences, and that people should be mindful of the potential risks or dangers of their behavior

word_list.word: first in, best dressed
word_list.pronunciation: fˈɜːst ˈɪn bˈɛst dɹˈɛst

senses.definition: used to imply that being proactive and taking early action gives someone an advantage in achieving success or obtaining what they want

word_list.word: what goes up must come down
word_list.pronunciation: wˌʌt ɡoʊz ˌʌp mˈʌst kˈʌm dˈaʊn

senses.definition: used to suggest that success, power, or fortune is often temporary and that anything that rises to a high position or level will eventually fall or decline

examples.x: "The economy is booming right now, but we have to be prepared for when things slow down because what goes up must come down," cautioned the economist.

word_list.word: necessity is the mother of invention
word_list.pronunciation: nəsˈɛsɪɾi ɪz ðə mˈʌðɚɹ ʌv ɪnvˈɛnʃən

senses.definition: used to say that when faced with a difficult situation or problem, people are motivated to find new solutions or innovations to overcome it

word_list.word: there is reason in the roasting of eggs
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz ɹˈiːzən ɪnðə ɹˈoʊstɪŋ ʌv ˈɛɡz

senses.definition: used to encourage individuals to be mindful of the significance of even small things and to recognize that there is often a deeper meaning or purpose behind them

word_list.word: one who handles honey, licks his fingers
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn hˌuː hˈændəlz hˈʌni lˈɪks hɪz fˈɪŋɡɚz

senses.definition: used to imply that those who are involved in something good, beneficial, or profitable are likely to enjoy the benefits themselves

word_list.word: all covet, all lose
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɔːl kˈʌvɪt ˈɔːl lˈuːz

senses.definition: used to imply that when people become too focused on acquiring something that is highly desired, it can lead to negative outcomes or loss for everyone involved
